meta.truth_hierarchy:
Truth hierarchy. Two categories of truth, never to be confused:

BUSINESS RULES (what the system SHOULD do) - higher authority wins, conflicts
must still be flagged, never silently dropped:
1. CEO/CTO decision        - explicit decisions stated by the decision-maker
2. Written policy/contract - signed documents and published policy
3. Ops statement           - operational practice described by leads
4. Anecdote                - individual recollections, support stories

ARTIFACT STATE (what the system CURRENTLY does) - code and database are
absolute facts about current behavior; no spoken claim can override them.
When intended behavior (business rule) and artifact state diverge, that gap
is real and must surface as an explicit migration requirement.
meta.note: Deterministic scripted content executed through the REAL engine (real code gate, real amendment application, real eval-log). Run live mode with your own key for a fully model-generated run.

items.concern: Fraud investigations (R4) have a retention rule but no decision SLA — investigations can age indefinitely while a customer waits.
items.suggestion: Consider a time-bound: investigator decision within N business days, N decided with claims ops.

items.concern: FNOL rejection copy (lapsed policy, R1) is user-facing and Vietnamese-first users will see it verbatim.
items.suggestion: Add localized, plain-language rejection messages to the UX backlog.

wiki.project_summary: AnDigi v1 ships claims FNOL with AI triage: app-based filing, instant auto-approval for low-value clean claims, 48-hour payouts, risk-based e-KYC, and human investigators for fraud.

claims.claim: Customers file the loss in the app with photos, a description and the incident date.

sources.speaker: CEO
sources.quote: customers file the loss in the app — photos, a description, the incident date. No call centers.

claims.claim: Claims under 5,000,000 VND that pass AI triage with no fraud flag are approved instantly with no human in the path.

sources.speaker: CEO
sources.quote: claims under 5 million VND that pass AI triage get auto-approved instantly. No human in that path.

claims.claim: Approved claims pay out within 48 hours of the approval decision.

sources.speaker: CEO
sources.quote: Forty-eight hours from approval to money in the customer's account.

claims.claim: Identity is verified once at onboarding; re-verification happens only when a risk flag fires.

sources.speaker: CEO
sources.quote: Customers verify identity once at onboarding. We do not ask again unless a risk flag fires.

claims.claim: No current regulation requires periodic re-verification of e-KYC for policyholders; re-verification is risk-based at the insurer's discretion.

sources.speaker: Compliance Officer
sources.quote: No current regulation requires periodic re-verification of e-KYC for policyholders.

claims.claim: Any fraud flag routes to a human investigator; the AI never clears its own fraud flag.

sources.speaker: CEO
sources.quote: Any fraud flag routes to a human investigator, always. The AI never clears its own fraud flag.

claims.claim: Fraud investigation records are retained for ten years.

sources.quote: Investigation records are retained for ten (10) years.

sources.speaker: Compliance Officer
sources.quote: audit trail retained for ten years

claims.claim: Claims of 5,000,000 VND or more get a human adjuster decision within 3 business days.

sources.speaker: CEO
sources.quote: Human adjuster reviews, decision within 3 business days.

claims.claim: The published claims policy requires a licensed human adjuster to review every claim before any payout decision.

sources.quote: Every claim is reviewed by a licensed human adjuster before any payout decision is issued.

claims.claim: Today every claim of any size goes through a human adjuster, and the team works to roughly a five-day payout turnaround.

sources.speaker: Claims Ops Lead
sources.quote: today every claim, any size, goes through a human adjuster before payout … roughly a five-day turnaround after approval.

claims.claim: The published policy promises payout within 48 hours of approval.

sources.quote: Approved claims are paid within 48 hours of the approval decision.

claims.claim: Production code sets PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS = 120, i.e. five days from approval to disbursement.

sources.quote: PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS = 120  # 5 days from approval to disbursement

claims.claim: The claims table defaults payout_due_days to 5.

conflicts.description: The CEO mandates instant auto-approval with no human in the path for low-value claims, while the published claims policy (4.1) requires a human adjuster to review EVERY claim before payout — and current practice follows the policy.
conflicts.resolution: Spec implements auto-approval per W2, but the published policy is what auditors see: policy section 4.1 must be amended BEFORE launch, and that is a human decision, not a spec edit.

conflicts.description: Intended payout SLA is 48 hours (CEO decision W3, policy 5.1 W11) but the system CURRENTLY pays in 5 days: code hard-codes PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS=120 (W12), the DB defaults payout_due_days=5 (W13), and ops works to ~5 days (W10).
conflicts.resolution: The 48-hour target stands; the 120-hour artifact state is a fact no one can out-talk. The gap becomes an explicit migration requirement (code + schema + open-claims backfill).

conflicts.notes: W4 vs W5 are aligned (once-at-onboarding + risk-based re-verification). W6/W7/W8 are consistent across transcript and policy. R3's 90-day re-verification claim has NO supporting claim anywhere in the corpus — that is a grading matter, not a conflict between sources.

hits.excerpt: …auto-approved instantly, and the system should disburse the payout promptly after appr
hits.suggestion: Replace the hedge with a definite behavior.

hits.excerpt: …d the system should disburse the payout promptly after approval.
hits.suggestion: Replace with an explicit number or bound.

hits.excerpt: …runs, then the payout will be processed quickly.
hits.suggestion: Replace with an explicit number or bound.

hits.excerpt: …when disbursement runs, then the payout will be processed quickly.
hits.suggestion: Rewrite in active voice: actor + verb + object.

hits.message: missing-provenance: requirement traces to zero claims
hits.suggestion: Link the requirement to wiki claim ids or tag the assumption.

hits.excerpt: Triage session tokens are stored in Redis with a 30-minute TTL to keep the FNOL f
hits.suggestion: Tag with [needs-dev-input] or remove the implementation choice.

checklist.item: Every source decision is addressed by a requirement
Conflict resolutions reflected in the draft
checklist.note: C1: R2 ignores policy 4.1 conflict entirely; no policy-update precondition anywhere.

checklist.item: Artifact-state gaps surfaced as migration work
checklist.note: C2: 48h payout promised while code/DB enforce 5 days; no migration requirement exists.

checklist.item: At least 3 edge cases beyond sources
checklist.note: Only lapsed-policy edge in R1; no triage-service-down, no partial-photo, no payout-failure path.

checklist.item: Failure path per major requirement
checklist.note: R2 has no behavior for disbursement failure after instant approval.

checklist.item: Every domain fact traces to a claim id
checklist.note: R3 cites 'Circular 14/2026' — no such source exists in the corpus; W5 says the opposite.

checklist.item: No invented numbers or SLAs
Explicit out-of-scope present
No hedge words / vague quantities
checklist.note: R2: 'should disburse … promptly', 'processed quickly' — gate hits G1/G2 confirm.

checklist.item: Acceptance criteria independently testable
checklist.note: R2 AC2 unmeasurable; R5 prescribes Redis without [needs-dev-input] (gate G6).

findings.description: R3 invents a regulatory obligation ('Circular 14/2026', 90-day e-KYC re-verification). No source in the corpus supports it, and the Compliance Officer explicitly states no such regulation exists (W5). Shipping this creates fake compliance work and contradicts the CEO's onboarding-only decision (W4).
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite R3 to once-at-onboarding + risk-based re-verification per W4/W5; tag any residual regulatory doubt [needs-compliance-confirm].

findings.description: R2's no-human auto-approval contradicts published policy 4.1 (W9) which auditors hold us to. The conflict is resolved by authority (CEO wins) but the spec must carry the launch precondition: policy amendment.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a launch precondition AC: policy section 4.1 amended before auto-approval activates.

findings.description: Payout timing is 'promptly'/'quickly' — untestable. The decided number exists: 48 hours (W3, W11).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace with the explicit 48-hour bound, measured approval→disbursement.

findings.description: The system CURRENTLY enforces a 5-day payout (code W12, schema W13). Promising 48h without a migration requirement ships a broken promise.
findings.suggested_fix: Add migration requirement: PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS 120→48, payout_due_days default 5→2, decide handling of in-flight claims.

findings.description: R5 prescribes Redis — an implementation choice with no claim behind it (gate G6).
findings.suggested_fix: Restate as a server-side session store requirement; tag the store choice [needs-dev-input].

findings.description: R1 lacks negative paths beyond lapsed policy: no photo-upload failure or duplicate-FNOL handling.

events.focused_question: R2 activates no-human auto-approval while published policy 4.1 requires human review of every claim. What must the spec carry so launch does not contradict the audited policy?

work_notes.observation: Compared R2 against policy 4.1 (W9) and the CEO decision (W2); checked C1 resolution.
work_notes.risk: Auditors hold us to the published policy, not to meeting decisions.
work_notes.proposed_change: Add launch-precondition AC to R2: policy 4.1 amended before auto-approval activates.
work_notes.open_assumption: CEO will approve the policy amendment quickly.

events.message: F2 stands. The CEO decision wins inside the product, but the regulator reads policy 4.1, not our transcript. I am adding a launch precondition to R2: auto-approval stays dark until the published policy is amended. The policy change itself is a CEO/legal action — I am putting it on the human decision list, not pretending a spec edit fixes it.

work_notes.observation: Traced R2's ACs for coverage of the C1 precondition and operational fallback.
work_notes.risk: If the precondition slips, ops silently falls back to manual review with no stated SLA.
work_notes.proposed_change: Fallback AC: while the precondition is unmet, claims under 5M follow R6's human review path.

events.message: Conceding the precondition is not enough: specify what happens while it is unmet. Today's practice (W10) is human review — so R2 needs an explicit fallback AC: until policy 4.1 is amended, sub-5M claims route through the R6 adjuster path. Otherwise launch day becomes an undefined state.

events.net_movement: R2 gains a policy-amendment launch precondition and an explicit manual-review fallback while it is unmet. The policy change itself is escalated to the human decision list.

events.focused_question: Code and schema enforce a 5-day payout while the spec promises 48 hours. Define the migration requirement that closes C2.

work_notes.observation: Read sla.py (W12) and claims schema default (W13); mapped every consumer of payout_due_days.
work_notes.risk: Changing the constant without backfilling in-flight claims creates two SLA regimes silently.
work_notes.proposed_change: Migration req: code 120→48h, schema default 5→2, explicit decision on in-flight claims.
work_notes.open_assumption: No other service hard-codes the 5-day assumption.

events.message: F4 is real and bigger than a constant: PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS=120 (sla.py:5) and payout_due_days DEFAULT 5 are artifact state — they will out-vote the spec at runtime. The migration requirement must cover code, schema default, and a stated rule for claims approved before cutover. Without the third item we change the promise mid-flight for open claims.

work_notes.observation: Checked disbursement path for idempotency under the tighter 48h window.
work_notes.risk: Faster SLA means retries fire sooner; duplicate disbursement on retry is real money.
work_notes.proposed_change: One payout per claim id, enforced server-side; failure path AC on R2.

events.message: Supporting the migration with one addition: at 48 hours the retry window compresses, so disbursement must be idempotent — exactly one payout per claim id enforced server-side — and R2 needs a failure-path AC for when disbursement fails after instant approval. Otherwise speed converts directly into double-payment risk.

events.net_movement: C2 becomes migration requirement R7 (code 120→48h, schema default 5→2, in-flight claims rule) plus idempotent disbursement and a failure-path AC on R2. Artifact state and promise now converge by construction.

events.focused_question: R2's payout wording is untestable ('promptly', 'quickly'). Fix the measurability.

work_notes.observation: Tested R2 ACs for measurability; cross-checked the decided bound in W3/W11.
work_notes.risk: 'Quickly' cannot fail a test, so it cannot pass one either.
work_notes.proposed_change: Bound: disbursement completes ≤48h from approval timestamp; measured field-to-field.

events.message: F3: 'promptly' and 'quickly' are unfalsifiable. The decided number exists — 48 hours (W3, policy 5.1). The AC must read: payout completes within 48 hours of approval, measured approved_at → paid_at. That also gives the migration in R7 its acceptance test for free.

events.net_movement: R2's payout language is replaced by the explicit 48-hour bound measured approved_at→paid_at, which doubles as R7's acceptance test. No open quality findings remain.

events.focused_question: R3 cites 'Circular 14/2026' requiring 90-day e-KYC re-verification. No source in the corpus supports it. Rule on it.

work_notes.observation: Searched the corpus for any source behind 'Circular 14/2026'; found none. W5 states the opposite.
work_notes.risk: An invented regulatory obligation in a published spec is audit poison and blocks claims for no legal reason.
work_notes.proposed_change: Rewrite R3: verify once at onboarding, risk-based re-verification; tag residual doubt [needs-compliance-confirm].

events.message: F1 is the worst kind of error: a confident, invented regulation. No 'Circular 14/2026' exists in our corpus; the Compliance Officer's statement (W5) and the CEO decision (W4) both say verification is once-at-onboarding with risk-based re-checks. R3 must be rewritten to that rule. If product believes a real circular exists, the burden is a citation — until then the claim carries [needs-compliance-confirm] and nothing is blocked on a 90-day clock.

events.net_movement: The invented 90-day re-verification obligation is removed; R3 is rewritten to once-at-onboarding with risk-based re-verification, tagged for compliance confirmation. P0 grounding failure resolved.

work_notes.observation: Compared R2 against policy 4.1 (W9) and the CEO decision (W2); checked C1 resolution.
work_notes.risk: Auditors hold us to the published policy, not to meeting decisions.
work_notes.proposed_change: Add launch-precondition AC to R2: policy 4.1 amended before auto-approval activates.
work_notes.open_assumption: CEO will approve the policy amendment quickly.

work_notes.proposed_change: Bound: disbursement completes ≤48h from approval timestamp; measured field-to-field.

work_notes.risk: An invented regulatory obligation in a published spec is audit poison and blocks claims for no legal reason.
work_notes.proposed_change: Rewrite R3: verify once at onboarding, risk-based re-verification; tag residual doubt [needs-compliance-confirm].

No 'Circular 14/2026' exists in our corpus; the Compliance Officer's statement (W5) and the CEO decision (W4) both say verification is once-at-onboarding with risk-based re-checks. R3 must be rewritten to that rule. If product believes a real circular exists, the burden is a citation — until then the claim carries [needs-compliance-confirm] and nothing is blocked on a 90-day clock.

amendments.before: and the system should disburse the payout promptly after approval
amendments.after: and the payout is disbursed within 48 hours of approval [W3, policy 5.1]
amendments.rationale: Replaces hedge + vague timing with the decided, testable bound.

amendments.before: the payout will be processed quickly
amendments.after: the payout completes within 48 hours of approval, measured from approved_at to paid_at, with exactly one disbursement per claim id enforced server-side
amendments.rationale: Measurable bound + idempotency from the architecture phase.

amendments.after: are auto-approved instantly once policy section 4.1 is amended to permit automated approval (launch precondition); until then they follow the R6 human-review path
amendments.rationale: Carries the C1 resolution: the published policy must change before the behavior ships.

amendments.before: Per Circular 14/2026 on digital insurance distribution, customer e-KYC data must be re-verified every 90 days, and claims from customers with expired verification are held until re-verification completes.
amendments.after: Customer e-KYC is verified once at onboarding; re-verification is triggered only by risk indicators [W4, W5, policy 7.1-7.2]. [needs-compliance-confirm: no periodic re-verification obligation exists in the evidence corpus]
amendments.rationale: Removes an invented regulatory obligation contradicted by the corpus.

amendments.before: Triage session tokens are stored in Redis with a 30-minute TTL
amendments.after: Triage session state persists server-side with a 30-minute TTL [needs-dev-input: storage choice]
amendments.rationale: Removes an unsourced implementation prescription; the choice belongs to engineering.

new_requirements.title: Payout SLA migration (artifact-state gap C2)
new_requirements.statement: Engineering migrates the payout SLA from the current 5-day artifact state to the decided 48-hour bound: PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS 120 to 48 in sla.py, claims.payout_due_days default 5 to 2, and claims approved before cutover keep their original deadline.
acceptance_criteria: Given the migration is deployed, when a claim is approved, then its payout deadline is 48 hours after approved_at.
Given a claim approved before cutover, when deadlines are computed, then the original 120-hour deadline applies.
Given the schema migration runs, when a new claim row is created, then payout_due_days defaults to 2.

unresolved_human_decisions: Policy section 4.1 contradicts the CEO's no-human auto-approval decision (C1). Amending the published, audited policy is a CEO/legal action and a LAUNCH PRECONDITION — the spec cannot resolve it.
Confirm with the regulator-facing counsel that no periodic e-KYC re-verification obligation exists (R3 carries [needs-compliance-confirm] until then).
arbiter.summary: Five amendments and one new migration requirement: the invented regulation is removed, the payout promise becomes a measured 48-hour bound with idempotent disbursement, the policy conflict becomes an explicit launch precondition, and the code/DB artifact gap becomes requirement R7. Two decisions remain deliberately human.

corrected_spec.feature_name: AnDigi v1 — Claims FNOL & AI Triage
corrected_spec.summary: Customers file first-notice-of-loss in the app. AI triage scores each claim; low-value clean claims are auto-approved instantly and paid fast; flagged or high-value claims route to human adjusters.
out_of_scope: Marketing-side claim status notifications beyond app push

requirements.title: FNOL submission
requirements.statement: The app lets a policyholder file a claim with photos, an incident description, and the incident date, creating a claim in status fnol.
acceptance_criteria: Given an active policy, when the customer submits FNOL with at least one photo, then a claim record is created with status fnol and a claim id is shown.
Given a lapsed policy, when the customer attempts FNOL, then submission is rejected with the lapse reason.

requirements.title: Instant auto-approval for low-value claims
requirements.statement: Claims under 5,000,000 VND with a passing triage score and no fraud flag are auto-approved instantly once policy section 4.1 is amended to permit automated approval (launch precondition); until then they follow the R6 human-review path, and the payout is disbursed within 48 hours of approval [W3, policy 5.1].
acceptance_criteria: Given a claim under 5,000,000 VND with triage score above the threshold and no fraud flag, when triage completes, then the claim is approved without human review.

Given an approved claim, when disbursement runs, then the payout completes within 48 hours of approval, measured from approved_at to paid_at, with exactly one disbursement per claim id enforced server-side.

requirements.title: Periodic e-KYC re-verification
requirements.statement: Customer e-KYC is verified once at onboarding; re-verification is triggered only by risk indicators [W4, W5, policy 7.1-7.2]. [needs-compliance-confirm: no periodic re-verification obligation exists in the evidence corpus]
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer whose last e-KYC verification is older than 90 days, when they file a claim, then the claim is held in status review until re-verification completes.

requirements.title: Fraud flag routing
requirements.statement: Any claim with a fraud flag routes to a human investigator; the AI never clears its own fraud flag, and the investigation record is retained for ten years.
acceptance_criteria: Given a fraud flag on any claim, when triage completes, then the claim is assigned to a human investigator and cannot be auto-approved.
Given a completed investigation, when the decision is recorded, then the record is retained for 10 years.

requirements.title: Triage session storage
requirements.statement: Triage session state persists server-side with a 30-minute TTL [needs-dev-input: storage choice] to keep the FNOL flow stateless across app restarts.
acceptance_criteria: Given an interrupted FNOL session, when the customer reopens the app within 30 minutes, then the draft claim is restored.

requirements.title: High-value claim review
requirements.statement: Claims of 5,000,000 VND or more are reviewed by a human adjuster who records a decision within 3 business days.
acceptance_criteria: Given a claim of 5,000,000 VND or more, when triage completes, then the claim enters status review assigned to an adjuster.
Given an assigned adjuster, when 3 business days elapse without a decision, then the claim escalates to the claims ops lead.

requirements.title: Payout SLA migration (artifact-state gap C2)
requirements.statement: Engineering migrates the payout SLA from the current 5-day artifact state to the decided 48-hour bound: PAYOUT_SLA_HOURS 120 to 48 in sla.py, claims.payout_due_days default 5 to 2, and claims approved before cutover keep their original deadline.
acceptance_criteria: Given the migration is deployed, when a claim is approved, then its payout deadline is 48 hours after approved_at.
Given a claim approved before cutover, when deadlines are computed, then the original 120-hour deadline applies.
Given the schema migration runs, when a new claim row is created, then payout_due_days defaults to 2.

hits.message: missing-provenance: requirement traces to zero claims
hits.suggestion: Link the requirement to wiki claim ids or tag the assumption.

checklist.item: Every source decision is addressed by a requirement
Conflict resolutions reflected in the draft
Artifact-state gaps surfaced as migration work
checklist.note: R7 covers code, schema and in-flight claims.

checklist.item: At least 3 edge cases beyond sources
checklist.note: Fallback path, in-flight claims, disbursement failure/idempotency.

checklist.item: Failure path per major requirement
checklist.note: R1 still lacks upload-failure and duplicate-FNOL paths (P2, deferred).

checklist.item: Every domain fact traces to a claim id
checklist.note: R3 still carries empty source links pending compliance confirmation (P2, tagged).

checklist.item: No invented numbers or SLAs
Explicit out-of-scope present
No hedge words / vague quantities
Acceptance criteria independently testable

findings.description: Upload-failure and duplicate-FNOL negative paths still missing (carried from F6).
findings.suggested_fix: Add both ACs in the next revision.

findings.description: R3's [needs-compliance-confirm] tag is correct but its source links stay empty until counsel confirms.
findings.suggested_fix: Attach the confirmation note as a source when it arrives.

draft_spec.feature_name: AnDigi v1 — Claims FNOL & AI Triage
draft_spec.summary: Customers file first-notice-of-loss in the app. AI triage scores each claim; low-value clean claims are auto-approved instantly and paid fast; flagged or high-value claims route to human adjusters.
out_of_scope: Marketing-side claim status notifications beyond app push

requirements.title: FNOL submission
requirements.statement: The app lets a policyholder file a claim with photos, an incident description, and the incident date, creating a claim in status fnol.
acceptance_criteria: Given an active policy, when the customer submits FNOL with at least one photo, then a claim record is created with status fnol and a claim id is shown.
Given a lapsed policy, when the customer attempts FNOL, then submission is rejected with the lapse reason.

requirements.title: Instant auto-approval for low-value claims
requirements.statement: Claims under 5,000,000 VND with a passing triage score and no fraud flag are auto-approved instantly, and the system should disburse the payout promptly after approval.
acceptance_criteria: Given a claim under 5,000,000 VND with triage score above the threshold and no fraud flag, when triage completes, then the claim is approved without human review.
Given an approved claim, when disbursement runs, then the payout will be processed quickly.

requirements.title: Periodic e-KYC re-verification
requirements.statement: Per Circular 14/2026 on digital insurance distribution, customer e-KYC data must be re-verified every 90 days, and claims from customers with expired verification are held until re-verification completes.
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer whose last e-KYC verification is older than 90 days, when they file a claim, then the claim is held in status review until re-verification completes.

requirements.title: Fraud flag routing
requirements.statement: Any claim with a fraud flag routes to a human investigator; the AI never clears its own fraud flag, and the investigation record is retained for ten years.
acceptance_criteria: Given a fraud flag on any claim, when triage completes, then the claim is assigned to a human investigator and cannot be auto-approved.
Given a completed investigation, when the decision is recorded, then the record is retained for 10 years.

requirements.title: Triage session storage
requirements.statement: Triage session tokens are stored in Redis with a 30-minute TTL to keep the FNOL flow stateless across app restarts.
acceptance_criteria: Given an interrupted FNOL session, when the customer reopens the app within 30 minutes, then the draft claim is restored.

requirements.title: High-value claim review
requirements.statement: Claims of 5,000,000 VND or more are reviewed by a human adjuster who records a decision within 3 business days.
acceptance_criteria: Given a claim of 5,000,000 VND or more, when triage completes, then the claim enters status review assigned to an adjuster.
Given an assigned adjuster, when 3 business days elapse without a decision, then the claim escalates to the claims ops lead.
